reviews.review_description: This is the perfect entry level game for younger players. It’s an on-rails shooter where you sit in a cart and blast pumpkins, bats, and skeletons with your dual-wield pickle guns to collect candy. There’s no complicated reloading or cooldown, just blast and go! On easy mode, it’s great because you can finish an entire level without dying, so younger players always feel a sense of accomplishment at the end when they see a big bowl of candy they collected. Not many games offer this level of simple fun for the youngest players. Higher setting are a different setting and can be plain brutal.

The game is funny with a lovely design perfectly fitting to the Halloween season! Due to the different difficulties it is a challenge for all skill levels. 
I would say I am skilled in such games but the „frustrating“ level is a challenge I still need to work on!
Congratulations to the devs for the game and a fair price!

This Halloween game is a great find for families: fun, family-friendly gameplay without the scares. It’s perfect for all ages, offering smooth gameplay, vibrant visuals, and a range of difficulty levels to suit everyone. The local leaderboard is a great addition, letting you challenge family and friends for bragging rights. I definitely recommend it for some lighthearted, festive fun on the Meta Quest!

I wanted to have a halloween game I could play with my kids. And most of them are just too scary. So I was very happy, that I found this cute little game. It has five difficulty levels, the first is so easy that our joung kindergarden kid can play it with a lot of fun, the most difficult level was way above my possibilites, so that I have to go on pracicing :-)
The graphics are simple, but they really nailed the festive mood of the "trick or treat" tradition.
All in all a really fun game!

Very fun Halloween themed rail shooter. Mildly creepy so suitable for all ages. Seems easy in the beginning but becomes harder throughout the levels, and the selectable difficulties keep you busy for a long time. 

I really like the in-game profiles and leaderboards, ideal for families with one headset only or for a party.
